sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports that her son has autism and she has been following up with a variety of specialists, including a neurologist, allergist, and ophthalmologist. She also reports that she took him off cow's milk and now provides him with Ripple protein milk. She states that she drinks a lot of water and juice, and she used to only drink milk. She reports that she has seen many wet diapers, around 8 to 10 per day. She is 21 months old and her twin is a girl who is about a pound heavier and slightly shorter. The patient lives in Dallas, Georgia.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient appears well-nourished, with mild hypotonia of the lower extremities. External genitalia are normal. Vital signs are within normal limits. Examination reveals small appearing eyes.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: Katherine is a 22-month-old female with Smith-Magenis syndrome, potentially affecting the kidneys and urinary tract. The patient's history suggests possible congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract, as reported in the literature associated with this syndrome. She is currently being evaluated for these abnormalities.
medical_concepts: congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: We will obtain a kidney ultrasound today to evaluate for congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract. If abnormalities are detected, we will determine the next steps and future follow-up. The family lives in Dallas, Georgia, so her follow-up should be at the Town Center location. The nurse will provide instructions for the ultrasound and complete necessary paperwork.
medical_concepts: congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract
congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract
